steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: ”Having climbed the charts for six weeks,” is a participial phrase. The blank serves as the subject of the independent clause that follows. The antecedent is “the song” (singular).

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content: Pronoun-antecedent agreement requires a singular pronoun for a singular antecedent; “it” satisfies this. “Those” and “they” are plural; “each” does not function as a subject pronoun. That matches choice D — D is correct.
